2603.20750.title: Modeling Epistemic Uncertainty in Social Perception via Rashomon Set Agents
2603.20750.paper_summary: We present an LLM-driven multi-agent probabilistic modeling framework that demonstrates how differences in students' subjective social perceptions arise and evolve in real-world classroom settings, under constraints from an observed social network and limited questionnaire data. When social information is incomplete and the accuracy of perception differs between students, they can form different views of the same group structure from local cues they can access. Repeated peer communication and belief updates can gradually change these views and, over time, lead to stable group-level differences. To avoid assuming a global "god's-eye view," we assign each student an individualized subjective graph that shows which social ties they can perceive and how far information is reachable from their perspective. All judgments and interactions are restricted to this subjective graph: agents use retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to access only local information and then form evaluations of peers' competence and social standing. We also add structural perturbations related to social-anxiety to represent consistent individual differences in the accuracy of social perception. During peer exchanges, agents share narrative assessments of classmates' academic performance and social position with uncertainty tags, and update beliefs probabilistically using LLM-based trust scores. Using the time series of six real exam scores as an exogenous reference, we run multi-step simulations to examine how epistemic uncertainty spreads through local interactions. Experiments show that, without relying on global information, the framework reproduces several collective dynamics consistent with real-world educational settings. The code is released at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/Rashomonomon-0126.
2603.20750.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個基於大型語言模型的多代理概率建模框架，展示了學生主觀社會感知的差異如何在現實課堂環境中產生和演變，這些差異受到觀察到的社交網絡和有限問卷數據的限制。當社交信息不完整且學生之間的感知準確性不同時，他們可以根據可訪問的局部線索形成對同一群體結構的不同看法。重複的同儕交流和信念更新可以逐漸改變這些看法，並隨著時間的推移導致穩定的群體層級差異。為了避免假設全球的「上帝視角」，我們為每位學生分配一個個性化的主觀圖，顯示他們可以感知哪些社交聯繫以及從他們的視角信息的可達距離。所有的判斷和互動都限制在這個主觀圖中：代理人使用檢索增強生成（RAG）僅訪問局部信息，然後對同儕的能力和社會地位進行評估。我們還添加了與社交焦慮相關的結構擾動，以代表社會感知準確性的持續個體差異。在同儕交流中，代理人分享對同學學業表現和社會地位的敘述評估，並附上不確定性標籤，並使用基於大型語言模型的信任分數以概率方式更新信念。利用六個真實考試成績的時間序列作為外生參考，我們進行多步模擬，以檢查認知不確定性如何通過局部互動擴散。實驗顯示，在不依賴全球信息的情況下，該框架再現了幾種與現實教育環境一致的集體動態。代碼已發布於 https://anonymous.4open.science/r/Rashomonomon-0126。
2603.20750.author: Jinming Yang et.al.
2603.20750.authors: Jinming Yang, Xinyu Jiang, Xinshan Jiao, Xinping Zhang
